# PhoenixmlDb Documentation

> PhoenixmlDb Documentation — documentation site

- [Home](/index.html): PhoenixmlDb — Modern XML/JSON document database for .NET
- [Release Notes](/release-notes.html): PhoenixmlDb version history and changelog

## getting-started

- [First Application](/getting-started/first-application.html): Build a complete library management app with PhoenixmlDb
- [Getting Started](/getting-started/index.html): Install PhoenixmlDb and run your first query in minutes
- [Installation](/getting-started/installation.html): Platform requirements, NuGet packages, and building from source
- [Quick Start](/getting-started/quick-start.html): Your first PhoenixmlDb query in 5 minutes

## language-reference

- [Language Reference](/language-reference/index.html): XPath, XSLT, and XQuery documentation for .NET developers
- [Document Structure](/language-reference/xml-foundations/document-structure.html): XML elements, attributes, and the document tree explained for JSON developers
- [XML Foundations](/language-reference/xml-foundations/index.html): XML essentials for developers coming from JSON
- [Namespaces](/language-reference/xml-foundations/namespaces.html): XML namespaces explained practically — when you need them and how to handle them
- [Unicode and .NET Requirements](/language-reference/xml-foundations/unicode-and-dotnet.html): ICU globalization requirements, Unicode normalization, collation, and platform setup for PhoenixmlDb
- [Working with JSON](/language-reference/xml-foundations/working-with-json.html): Parsing, querying, transforming, and outputting JSON with XPath, XSLT, and XQuery
- [The XQuery and XPath Data Model](/language-reference/xml-foundations/xdm.html): How XPath, XSLT, and XQuery see your XML documents
- [XML vs JSON](/language-reference/xml-foundations/xml-vs-json.html): Side-by-side comparison of XML and JSON for developers who think in JSON
- [Data Types](/language-reference/xpath/data-types.html): XPath's type system — atomic types, sequences, type casting, and schema interaction
- [Array Functions](/language-reference/xpath/functions/array.html): XPath ordered array operations — like List<T> in .NET
- [Boolean Functions](/language-reference/xpath/functions/boolean.html): XPath boolean construction and logic functions
- [Date and Time Functions](/language-reference/xpath/functions/date-time.html): XPath date/time construction, extraction, formatting, and timezone handling
- [Formatting Functions](/language-reference/xpath/functions/formatting.html): XPath number formatting, date formatting, serialization, and URI handling
- [Higher-Order Functions](/language-reference/xpath/functions/higher-order.html): XPath functions that take functions — map, filter, fold, and more
- [Functions](/language-reference/xpath/functions/index.html): Comprehensive reference for XPath 4.0's 240+ built-in functions
- [Map Functions](/language-reference/xpath/functions/map.html): XPath map operations — key-value data structures like Dictionary in .NET
- [Math Functions](/language-reference/xpath/functions/math.html): XPath math functions — trigonometry, logarithms, exponents, and constants
- [Node Functions](/language-reference/xpath/functions/node.html): XPath node properties, names, namespaces, and document access functions
- [Numeric Functions](/language-reference/xpath/functions/numeric.html): XPath numeric functions — arithmetic, rounding, aggregation
- [Sequence Functions](/language-reference/xpath/functions/sequence.html): XPath sequence operations — filtering, ordering, set operations, scanning
- [String Functions](/language-reference/xpath/functions/string.html): XPath string manipulation, searching, regex, Unicode, and URI functions
- [Type Constructors](/language-reference/xpath/functions/type-constructors.html): XPath type casting and XSD type constructor functions
- [XPath](/language-reference/xpath/index.html): XPath query language for XML — the LINQ of the XML world
- [Operators and Comparisons](/language-reference/xpath/operators.html): XPath arithmetic, comparison, and logical operators with important semantic differences from C#
- [Path Expressions](/language-reference/xpath/path-expressions.html): Navigate XML trees with XPath path expressions, axes, and predicates
- [Conditionals and Error Handling](/language-reference/xquery/conditionals.html): if/then/else, switch, typeswitch, try/catch, and the otherwise operator
- [Constructors](/language-reference/xquery/constructors.html): Building new XML elements, attributes, and documents in XQuery
- [Extensibility](/language-reference/xquery/extensibility.html): Extending XQuery with custom functions, modules, external functions, and .NET integration
- [FLWOR Expressions](/language-reference/xquery/flwor.html): XQuery's For-Let-Where-Order-Return expressions — LINQ query syntax for XML
- [Advanced FLWOR](/language-reference/xquery/flwor-advanced.html): Window clauses, count, group by, while, otherwise — advanced FLWOR patterns
- [Full-Text Search](/language-reference/xquery/full-text.html): XQuery Full-Text — ft:contains, match options, scoring, and search expressions
- [Functions and Modules](/language-reference/xquery/functions-modules.html): Writing reusable XQuery functions and organizing code into modules
- [XQuery](/language-reference/xquery/index.html): Query and construct XML with XQuery — SQL for hierarchical data
- [Operators](/language-reference/xquery/operators.html): XQuery comparison, arithmetic, sequence, and navigation operators
- [Prolog and Declarations](/language-reference/xquery/prolog.html): XQuery prolog — module declarations, namespace imports, options, and configuration
- [Type System](/language-reference/xquery/types.html): XQuery types, sequence types, records, enums, union types, and type testing
- [Update Facility](/language-reference/xquery/update-facility.html): XQuery Update — insert, delete, replace, rename, and transform expressions
- [XQuery vs XSLT](/language-reference/xquery/xquery-vs-xslt.html): When to use XQuery and when to use XSLT — a practical decision guide
- [Extensibility](/language-reference/xslt/extensibility.html): Extending XSLT with custom functions, packages, extension instructions, and .NET integration
- [Your First Transform](/language-reference/xslt/first-transform.html): Hands-on introduction to XSLT — transforming XML to HTML step by step
- [XSLT](/language-reference/xslt/index.html): Transform XML documents with XSLT — the declarative transformation language
- [String Analysis](/language-reference/xslt/instructions/analyze-string.html): xsl:analyze-string — regex-based string processing in XSLT
- [Control Flow](/language-reference/xslt/instructions/control-flow.html): xsl:if, xsl:choose, xsl:switch, xsl:where-populated, xsl:on-empty
- [Copying and Identity Transform](/language-reference/xslt/instructions/copying-identity.html): xsl:copy, xsl:copy-of, and the identity transform pattern
- [Dynamic Evaluation](/language-reference/xslt/instructions/dynamic-evaluation.html): xsl:evaluate — runtime XPath evaluation from string expressions
- [Error Handling](/language-reference/xslt/instructions/error-handling.html): xsl:try/catch, xsl:assert, xsl:fallback — handling errors and asserting conditions
- [User-Defined Functions](/language-reference/xslt/instructions/functions.html): xsl:function — defining reusable functions callable from XPath expressions
- [Grouping](/language-reference/xslt/instructions/grouping.html): xsl:for-each-group — all four grouping modes with examples
- [Instructions Reference](/language-reference/xslt/instructions/index.html): Complete reference for all XSLT 3.0/4.0 instructions
- [Iteration and Sorting](/language-reference/xslt/instructions/iteration-sorting.html): xsl:for-each, xsl:sort, xsl:perform-sort, xsl:iterate with break and next-iteration
- [Keys and Cross-References](/language-reference/xslt/instructions/keys.html): xsl:key — indexing documents for fast lookup with key() function
- [Maps, Arrays, and Records](/language-reference/xslt/instructions/maps-arrays.html): xsl:map, xsl:array, xsl:record, xsl:for-each-member — structured data in XSLT 3.0/4.0
- [Merging](/language-reference/xslt/instructions/merge.html): xsl:merge — combining multiple sorted input sources into a single output
- [Multiple Output Documents](/language-reference/xslt/instructions/multiple-outputs.html): xsl:result-document and xsl:output — generating multiple files and controlling serialization
- [Node Construction](/language-reference/xslt/instructions/node-construction.html): xsl:element, xsl:attribute, xsl:namespace, xsl:document — building nodes with dynamic names
- [Number Formatting](/language-reference/xslt/instructions/number-formatting.html): xsl:number, xsl:decimal-format, xsl:character-map — numbering and format customization
- [Output Instructions](/language-reference/xslt/instructions/output.html): xsl:value-of, xsl:text, xsl:comment, xsl:processing-instruction, xsl:sequence, xsl:message
- [Packages](/language-reference/xslt/instructions/packages.html): xsl:use-package, xsl:accept, xsl:override, xsl:expose — reusable stylesheet libraries
- [Streaming and Accumulators](/language-reference/xslt/instructions/streaming.html): xsl:source-document, xsl:accumulator, xsl:fork — processing large documents efficiently
- [Variables and Parameters](/language-reference/xslt/instructions/variables-parameters.html): xsl:variable, xsl:param, static parameters, tunnel parameters, with-param
- [Output Methods](/language-reference/xslt/output-methods.html): Producing HTML, XML, JSON, CSV, and text from XSLT transforms
- [Template Matching](/language-reference/xslt/template-matching.html): How XSLT template rules match nodes and produce output — the declarative paradigm shift

## phoenixmldb

- [Containers API](/phoenixmldb/api-reference/containers.html): Container creation, configuration, operations, and management
- [Documents API](/phoenixmldb/api-reference/documents.html): Document storage, retrieval, metadata, bulk operations, and error handling
- [API Reference](/phoenixmldb/api-reference/index.html): PhoenixmlDb .NET API — interfaces, classes, and usage patterns
- [Indexes API](/phoenixmldb/api-reference/indexes.html): Index creation, management, statistics, and query optimization
- [Queries API](/phoenixmldb/api-reference/queries.html): XQuery execution, parameters, results, explain plans, and prepared queries
- [Transactions API](/phoenixmldb/api-reference/transactions.html): Transaction lifecycle, commit/rollback, error handling, and patterns
- [XSLT API](/phoenixmldb/api-reference/xslt-api.html): XsltTransformer .NET API — transform XML with XSLT from C#
- [Configuration](/phoenixmldb/configuration.html): Database options, environment variables, logging, and connection strings
- [Core Concepts](/phoenixmldb/core-concepts.html): Containers, documents, the XDM, and shredded node storage
- [Database Extensions](/phoenixmldb/database-extensions.html): Custom XQuery functions, dbxml:metadata(), and extension development
- [Cluster Mode](/phoenixmldb/deployment/cluster-mode.html): Multi-node deployment with Raft consensus, sharding, and distributed transactions
- [Embedded Mode](/phoenixmldb/deployment/embedded-mode.html): Run PhoenixmlDb as an in-process library — no separate server needed
- [Deployment](/phoenixmldb/deployment/index.html): Run PhoenixmlDb as an embedded library, standalone server, or distributed cluster
- [Server Mode](/phoenixmldb/deployment/server-mode.html): Standalone server with gRPC API, authentication, TLS, and Docker deployment
- [Documents & Storage](/phoenixmldb/documents-and-storage.html): Storing and retrieving XML and JSON documents in containers
- [PhoenixmlDb](/phoenixmldb/index.html): Modern embedded XML/JSON document database for .NET
- [Indexing](/phoenixmldb/indexing.html): Path, value, full-text, structural, name, and metadata indexes
- [JSON Support](/phoenixmldb/json-support/index.html): Store, query, and index JSON documents with PhoenixmlDb
- [JSON Indexing](/phoenixmldb/json-support/json-indexing.html): How JSON documents get full indexing through the XML storage path
- [JSON Queries](/phoenixmldb/json-support/json-queries.html): XQuery patterns for querying JSON documents stored in PhoenixmlDb
- [JSON Storage](/phoenixmldb/json-support/json-storage.html): How JSON documents are stored, the XML mapping, and storage options
- [LINQ Provider](/phoenixmldb/linq-provider.html): Query PhoenixmlDb with LINQ — XML navigation, fluent API, and type mapping
- [Migration Guide](/phoenixmldb/migration-guide.html): Migrate from Berkeley DB XML, eXist-db, MarkLogic, MongoDB, or SQL
- [Mobile Development](/phoenixmldb/mobile-development.html): PhoenixmlDb on .NET MAUI, iOS, Android, and offline-first patterns
- [Performance Tuning](/phoenixmldb/performance-tuning.html): Query optimization, storage tuning, memory management, and monitoring
- [Transactions](/phoenixmldb/transactions.html): ACID transactions with MVCC via LMDB
- [Troubleshooting](/phoenixmldb/troubleshooting.html): Common issues and solutions for storage, queries, transactions, and clusters
- [XDM Data Model](/phoenixmldb/xdm-data-model.html): The XQuery Data Model — how PhoenixmlDb represents documents internally

## tools

- [Crucible](/tools/crucible.html): Static documentation generator — Markdown to XML to XSLT to HTML
- [Tools](/tools/index.html): PhoenixmlDb command-line tools — xslt, xquery, and crucible
- [xquery CLI](/tools/xquery-cli.html): Command-line XQuery processor — query XML documents from the terminal
- [xslt CLI](/tools/xslt-cli.html): Command-line XSLT 3.0/4.0 processor — transform XML documents from the terminal
